stories.headline: Ukraine asks Israel to seize vessel it claims is carrying grain stolen by Russia
stories.summary: Accusation vessel contains grain looted from Russian-occupied territories triggers diplomatic spat between both nations Ukraine has asked Israel to seize a vessel it claims is carrying grain looted from Russian-occupied territories, triggering a rare diplomatic spat between the two countries.The dispute spilt into public view this week when president Volodymyr Zelenskyy said that “another vessel” carrying grain “stolen by Russia” had arrived at a

stories.headline: US Oil Exports Hit Record as Iran War Energy Crunch Deepens
stories.summary: US crude exports surged to a record last week as the Iran war sends overseas buyers hunting for replacements to Middle Eastern oil.

stories.headline: U.S. aircraft carrier to leave Mideast, reducing military might amid Iran war
stories.summary: The USS Gerald R. Ford, at sea for 10 months, is in need of repair. Its exit, though, reduces the firepower on hand as Trump presses Tehran to make peace.

stories.headline: Komatsu expects profits to tumble 16% on Iran war, US tariffs
Young Palestinian artists from Gaza exhibit work shaped by war
stories.summary: In Gaza’s Bureij refugee camp, 64 young Palestinian artists have been showcasing works shaped by years of war.

stories.headline: Supreme Court limits key provision of the landmark Voting Rights Act
stories.summary: The decision could touch off a scramble by Republicans to redraw majority-minority congressional districts, especially in the South, that could cost many Black Democrats their seats.

stories.headline: Israeli 'double-tap' strike kills three rescue workers in Lebanon, officials say
stories.summary: They are among nine people killed in successive attacks on Tuesday, which Israel's military says targeted a Hezbollah commander.

stories.headline: Trump Argues UAE’s OPEC Exit Could Lower Energy Prices Amid War
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump said the United Arab Emirates’ decision to leave OPEC would help lower energy prices, which have spiked due to the war in Iran and continue to climb.

stories.headline: What to Know About the Voting Rights Act
stories.summary: The 1965 law was mean to address fundamental inequities in American life, and was one of the signal accomplishments of the civil rights movement.

stories.headline: Second US ambassador to Ukraine steps down in less than one year
stories.summary: The acting US ambassador to Ukraine is to step down from her post in Kyiv and retire, the US State Department announced. Reports have indicated her reasons for leaving the job are related to the Trump administration's approach to Ukraine.

stories.headline: Calls for humanitarian corridor through strait of Hormuz as Iran war hits vital aid
stories.summary: Soaring oil prices and the blockade are preventing food, fuel and medicine being delivered to millions of people in desperate need, say NGOsThe volatility of global oil prices caused by the US and Israel’s war on Iran is taking a toll on the most vulnerable people, by slowing or blocking food and medical aid from reaching them.Now aid organisations are calling for a “humanitarian corridor” to be opened through the strait of Hormuz amid rocketing

stories.headline: EU farmers and hauliers to get up to €50,000 to cover extra costs of Iran war
stories.summary: Fishing companies can also access subsidies in loosening of state aid rules to cover fuel and fertiliser price risesThe EU is to subsidise up to 70% of the extra cost of fuel and fertilisers caused by the Iran war for farmers, fishing businesses and road hauliers as part of a package of emergency measures.Individual companies can claim up to €50,000 each between now and the end of the year with minimum paperwork, a measure the EU hopes will remov

stories.headline: Iran’s currency falls to new low as US blockade, sanctions impact trade
stories.summary: The war has affected trade with some of Iran's largest partners, including China and the UAE.

stories.headline: EU loosens state aid rules as Middle East crisis hits energy costs
stories.summary: Brussels loosens state aid rules as Middle East turmoil drives up fuel and fertiliser costs, allowing governments to cushion farmers, hauliers and industry with emergency subsidies.

stories.headline: Ukraine formally asks Israel to seize vessel carrying grain stolen by Russia
stories.summary: A Ukrainian source told Euronews that "if Israel responded to other requests via diplomatic channels, there would have been no need for tweets," after Israel's foreign minister lashed out over what he called "Twitter diplomacy."

stories.headline: Madagascar detains French national over alleged plot to stir unrest
stories.summary: Malagasy prosecuters used a Whatsapp group as evidence for the detained individuals alleged crimes.

stories.headline: New images show suspect taking selfies before Washington press dinner shooting
stories.summary: Prosecutors argue Cole Tomas Allen should remain in custody until trial on a charge of attempted assassination of President Donald Trump.

stories.headline: Russia scales back Moscow Victory Day parade, blaming threat from Ukraine
stories.summary: Russia says the annual commemoration of Soviet victory in World War Two will not feature military vehicles or cadets.

stories.headline: Outrage after Indian man carries his sister's skeleton to a bank to prove her death
stories.summary: A video from Odisha state of Jitu Munda taking the remains to the bank went viral this week, leading to anger.

stories.headline: Oil surge divides Fed as Jay Powell’s term at helm draws to a close
stories.summary: Central bank chair says he will remain a governor ‘for a period of time’ after Kevin Warsh succeeds him

stories.headline: US Bonds Slump as Fed Divide Spurs Trader Bets on 2027 Rate Hike
stories.summary: US Treasuries held onto losses after the Federal Reserve’s decision to hold interest rates steady revealed a rift among policymakers regarding the economic fallout of the war in the Middle East.

stories.headline: Florida Lawmakers Were Debating New Congressional Map When Supreme Court Voting Rights Decision Landed
stories.summary: The map would eliminate as many as four Democratic-held districts, including one that is heavily Puerto Rican. Republicans expressed confidence in the map’s legality.

stories.headline: Supreme Court Strikes Down Louisiana Map, Another Blow to Voting Rights Act
stories.summary: The court struck down the voting map as an unconstitutional racial gerrymander in a move that could make it harder for lawmakers to create majority-minority voting districts.

stories.headline: Hot weather and hungry datacentres lift Australia’s energy demand to record highs but batteries quell prices
stories.summary: Rise in electricity demand in first quarter of 2026 was moderated by record output from rooftop solarGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastMore datacentres and warmer conditions helped push electricity demand to record highs in the first three months of the year, according to Australia’s Energy Market Operator, while growth in batteries kept average wholesale prices down.Electricity demand – from households, business and indu

stories.headline: Man carries deceased sister into Indian bank amid paperwork difficulty
stories.summary: A man in India was recorded carrying the remains of his deceased sister into a bank after he couldn't access her account

stories.headline: Madagascar detains French ex-serviceman over alleged destabilisation plot
stories.summary: French national Guy Baret is detained alongside Malagasy army officer over alleged conspiracy to destabilise Madagascar.

stories.headline: Senate panel advances Kevin Warsh’s nomination for US Fed chair
stories.summary: Senate panel approves Trump’s nominee Kevin Warsh to replace Jerome Powell as US Fed chair on party-line vote.

stories.headline: Italy says it has filed to be civil party in probe into Swiss bar fire that killed 41
stories.summary: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has vowed justice for Italian victims while Italy has opened its own investigation into the fire even as the Swiss probe continues.

stories.headline: London Stock Exchange adds first Ukrainian firms to reconstruction fund
stories.summary: For the first time Ukrainian equities have been included in a London-listed fund, which aims to channel investment into the nation’s eventual post-war reconstruction and current economic resilience.

stories.headline: Irish government announces further fuel supports after protests
stories.summary: The Irish government has announced further fuel supports for a number of sectors after nationwide protests in recent weeks.

stories.headline: United Arab Emirates to quit oil cartel Opec
stories.summary: The UAE's decision, after nearly 60 years of membership, is seen as a potential death knell for the oil cartel.

stories.headline: How Jay Powell repelled Donald Trump’s attacks on the Federal Reserve

stories.headline: King Charles, America and the futility of growth

stories.headline: Trump and PGA Tour reunite under gold statue at Doral

stories.headline: Musk says he was ‘a fool’ to fund the launch of OpenAI

stories.headline: Sun Pharma, Lilly Lead Latest Healthcare Deal Rush
stories.summary: Bloomberg's Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Deals" wraps up recent pharma dealmaking. Eli Lilly agreed to buy closely held Ajax Therapeutics for as much as $2.3 billion in cash. Sun Pharmaceutical Industries has agreed to acquire New York-listed women’s health-care company Organon. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Fed Leaves Rates Unchanged, Four Officials Dissent
stories.summary: The Federal Reserve leaves interest rates unchanged in a range of 3.5% to 3.75%. But four officials voted against the decision. It’s the first time since 1992 that there were four dissents. Bloomberg’s Michael McKee reports.

stories.headline: Kone Agrees to Buy TK Elevator for $34.4B
stories.summary: Bloomberg's Liana Baker joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." Kone agreed to acquire TK Elevator for $34.4 billion including debt, transforming the Finnish company into the industry’s dominant player and putting new pressure on its rivals. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Bill Ackman’s Pershing Square USA Sinks 16% After $5 Billion IPO
stories.summary: Shares of Bill Ackman’s Pershing Square USA Ltd. opened 16% below their IPO price, after the combined initial public offering for the closed-end fund and his alternative asset manager raised $5 billion.

stories.headline: Pernod Ricard and Jack Daniel’s Maker End Merger Discussions
stories.summary: Bloomberg's Michelle Davis joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." Jack Daniel’s owner Brown-Forman and Jameson whiskey maker Pernod Ricard SA terminated their merger talks, marking an abrupt end to a potential transatlantic deal that was aimed at contending with the industry’s downturn. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: 80 Seconds of Big Tech Earnings Will Decide Stock Market’s Fate
stories.summary: Investors looking for clues on which direction the stock market is headed in the coming weeks will get a rapid-fire reading as soon as trading ends on Wednesday.

stories.headline: King Charles urges checks on executive power as Trump hosts royal visit
stories.summary: In a speech to Congress, the monarch avoided naming the president but highlighted democratic norms, Ukraine and Western unity.

stories.headline: UAE quits OPEC: What that means for the Gulf, energy markets and beyond
stories.summary: UAE signalling intent to pursue independent economic policies and reshape Gulf oil politics.

stories.headline: Logistics pressure builds across Caspian-Central Asia corridor as trade routes evolve
stories.summary: The Caspian-Central Asia corridor is facing growing strain as rising trade volumes outpace existing pipelines, rail and port capacity, while the Middle Corridor gains relevance as an alternative route between Asia and Europe.

stories.summary: It will have little effect on the current oil blockades, but it could change everything afterwards.

stories.headline: Stocks and Bonds Fall as Fed Split Amid War Risks: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Stocks joined bonds lower as a divided Federal Reserve held interest rates steady and signaled the war in Iran is clouding the economic outlook. Brent crude hit the highest since 2022.

These followed the 21 April hijacking of Honour 25, a motor tanker c

stories.headline: Third Ukrainian strike hits Russian oil refinery and prompts evacuations
stories.summary: Residents near a burning refinery in Tuapse are told to leave, and the Kremlin accuses Kyiv of destabilising global energy markets.

stories.headline: Indian billionaire's son offers to save Escobar's hippos
stories.summary: Anant Ambani - son of Asia's richest man Mukesh Ambani - says he can take care of the animals roaming free since the drug lord's death.

stories.headline: Japan Airlines trials humanoid robots as ground handlers
stories.summary: These robots may in future help clean cabins and operate ground support equipment.

stories.headline: US soldier accused of betting on Maduro's removal pleads not guilty to fraud charges
stories.summary: The Army officer has been accused of using classified information to win $400,000 on an online prediction market.

stories.headline: Goldman stops bankers using Anthropic’s Claude in Hong Kong
stories.summary: Employees were unable to access company’s AI models as of a few weeks ago

stories.headline: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
stories.summary: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.

stories.headline: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.headline: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
stories.summary: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

stories.headline: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
stories.summary: Local elections have been held in the occupied West Bank and in one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not taking part.

stories.headline: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
stories.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.

stories.headline: 'We cried together': Trump's deportation drive forces tough decisions for couples
stories.summary: The spouses of illegal immigrants facing deportation must choose between separation or moving to a new country.

stories.headline: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire

stories.summary: Amjad Youssef was wanted over the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 in the district of Damascus.

stories.headline: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer
stories.summary: The Israeli prime minister says he is "in excellent physical condition" after having a malignant tumour removed.

stories.headline: Shooting sparks safety fears ahead of World Cup

stories.headline: Trump says Israel-Lebanon ceasefire to be extended by three weeks

stories.headline: US and Iran in blockade standoff as Pakistan pushes for talks
stories.summary: The mood in the Strait of Hormuz remains combustible despite Trump's ceasefire extension.

stories.headline: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
stories.summary: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time.
